1.1.name: Nationhood and Citizenship
1.1.definition: Effects on inclusion, equal recognition, or protection of minorities and marginalised groups.
1.1.description: Effects on the inclusivity of citizenship and the equal recognition and protection of minorities, vulnerable groups, and those at the margins of political life — including whether all people are equally seen, served, and protected by the state, or whether existing patterns of exclusion and discrimination are entrenched or deepened.

1.2.name: Rule of Law and Access to Justice
1.2.definition: Effects on legal processes, due process, and citizens' ability to obtain fair treatment and redress.
1.2.description: Effects on the integrity of legal processes, judicial independence, and citizens' ability to obtain fair treatment and redress; on whether public officials and institutions are subject to transparent and consistently applied rules; and on whether accountability for harms is clear and enforceable.

1.3.name: Civil and Political Rights
1.3.definition: Effects on freedoms of expression, assembly, or association, including through surveillance or censorship.
1.3.description: Effects on the freedoms of expression, movement, association, and assembly; on freedom from physical violation or fear; on freedom from surveillance, profiling, and censorship; and on the protection of those who defend human rights and democratic values.

1.4.name: Economic and Social Rights
1.4.definition: Effects on access to work, material conditions, or distribution of economic power.
1.4.description: Effects on access to work, social security, and basic necessities; on health and education; and on the broad and equitable distribution of economic gains across society.

2.1.name: Free and Fair Elections
2.1.definition: Effects on electoral integrity, voter access, campaign fairness, or trust in election outcomes.
2.1.description: Effects on the integrity of electoral processes, the inclusivity and accessibility of voting, the fairness of political competition, and public trust in elections as genuinely open contests.

2.2.name: Democratic Role of Political Parties
2.2.definition: Effects on political competition, party financing, or the balance between parties.
2.2.description: Effects on the freedom of parties to form, recruit and campaign; and on whether political competition between parties remains fair and free from undue external influence or structural advantage.

2.3.name: Effective and Accountable Government
2.3.definition: Effects on transparency, accountability, or fairness of government services and policymaking.
2.3.description: Effects on the capacity of government to deliver public services fairly and implement policy faithfully; on the competence, transparency, and integrity of public administration; and on whether the systems and technologies governments use to exercise state power remain open to democratic scrutiny — including where dependencies on private or foreign providers shift effective control over state functions beyond democratic reach.

2.4.name: Democratic Effectiveness of Legislature
2.4.definition: Effects on legislative scrutiny, oversight capacity, or executive-legislature power balance.
2.4.description: Effects on the capacity of parliament and other democratic institutions to scrutinise legislation and hold the executive to account; on the independence of the legislature from the executive; and on their capacity to receive and process genuine input.

2.5.name: Democratic Role of the Judiciary
2.5.definition: Effects on judicial independence, constitutional oversight of government, and citizens' ability to challenge the exercise of public power through legal means (strategic litigation).
2.5.description: Effects on the capacity of courts to provide independent oversight of the executive and legislature; on judicial review of laws and government decisions; and on the ability of citizens to challenge the exercise of public power through legal means.

2.6.name: Civilian Control of Military and Police
2.6.definition: Effects on oversight and accountability of security and enforcement agencies.
2.6.description: Effective civilian control over armed forces and security services; public accountability of police; absence of unaccountable private security actors.

2.7.name: Illegitimate Influence over Policy-Making
2.7.definition: Effects on corruption, regulatory capture, or undue private influence over public policy and the policy process.
2.7.description: Effects on the extent to which powerful actors shape policy through back channels — such as hidden lobbying, capturing regulators, or flooding consultations with artificial responses — rather than through open and accountable processes, and on how far policy outputs and outcomes reflect what the public actually wants.

3.1.name: Independent and Fact-Based Journalistic Media
3.1.definition: Effects on the independence, viability, and reach of regulated, professionally produced journalism.
3.1.description: Effects on the independence and pluralism of regulated, professional journalism; on the representativeness of opinion and the investigative capacity of journalism; on journalists' ability to operate freely; and on the accountability of platforms for the information flows they shape and amplify.

3.2.name: Opinion Formation and Political Participation
3.2.definition: Effects on how citizens form and exchange political views, participate in public life, or organise through civil society.
3.2.description: Effects on the conditions under which citizens form, exchange, and revise political views — including through social media and other non-journalistic channels; on the depth, breadth, and authenticity of citizen participation in public life; on equal access to public voice across social groups; and on the range and independence of civil society and social movements.

3.3.name: National–Subnational Power Relations
3.3.definition: Effects on the distribution of power between central and local government.
3.3.description: Effects on the independence and capacity of sub-central tiers of government; on local accountability and responsiveness to citizens; and on the distribution of power and resources between central and local government.

4.1.name: External Influences on Democracy
4.1.definition: Effects on foreign interference or the democratic-autocratic geopolitical balance.
4.1.description: Effects on a country's freedom from external interference in its democratic processes; on the geopolitical balance between democratic and autocratic models of governance; and on the ability of democratic states to maintain autonomous control over their political institutions and infrastructure.

4.2.name: Democratic Impact Abroad
4.2.definition: Effects of a country's AI policies or exports on democracy in other countries.
4.2.description: Effects on a country's support for human rights and democracy in other countries; on respect for international rule of law; and on whether a country's policies, exports, and international conduct strengthen or undermine democratic governance elsewhere.
